name: Ribhus
description: The Ribhus, also known as the Rbhus, are a group of divine artisans in Hindu mythology, particularly prominent in the Rigveda. They are the sons of Sudhanvan, a celestial being. The Ribhus are known for their exceptional craftsmanship, skill, and dedication to their work. Their most famous feat is transforming a single chalice made by their parents into four, thus demonstrating their superior abilities and benefiting humanity. This act of ingenuity and generosity earned them immortality and elevated them to the status of gods. They are often associated with Indra, the king of the gods, and are considered his close companions. The Ribhus are revered for their artistry, their ability to transform and improve things, and their benevolent nature towards humans. They represent the ideals of hard work, dedication, and the transformative power of skill. Their story highlights the importance of craftsmanship and the rewards that come with exceptional talent and service to others. They are also associated with youth, vigor, and the refreshing aspects of life. The Rigveda describes them as skilled chariot builders and armourers, further emphasizing their role as divine artisans. They are closely tied to the concepts of Soma and sacrifice, often being invoked during rituals. In some texts, they are identified with the seasons or aspects of nature, reflecting their transformative power over the natural world.
subtitle: Divine Artisans of Skill and Transformation

keyMyths.title: The Division of the Chalice
keyMyths.description: The Ribhus transformed a single chalice into four, showcasing their exceptional craftsmanship and earning them immortality. This act benefited both the gods and humanity.

keyMyths.title: Association with Indra
keyMyths.description: The Ribhus are close companions of Indra, often assisting him in his endeavors and sharing in the Soma offerings. Their loyalty and skill are highly valued by the king of the gods.

keyMyths.title: Crafting Divine Objects
keyMyths.description: The Ribhus are skilled artisans who create divine objects, including chariots and armor for the gods. Their craftsmanship is unparalleled, making them essential figures in the divine realm.

sources.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
